prompt_1:
When in doubt, rule in favor of the rider. Only rate FAIL if you are certain.

Apply the following rules to this parking photo:

1. The vehicle must be fully visible and uncropped. The photo must include enough surrounding space to judge the parking context. If the vehicle is cut off, rate FAIL.

2. Vehicles must not obstruct pedestrian flow. Parking to the side of a sidewalk without blocking the path is ok.

3. Parking in the furniture zone is preferred — this is the strip between the sidewalk and the street, often containing trees, benches, lampposts, or utility poles. Parking on adjacent grass is also acceptable.

4. A designated micromobility space is a white rectangle marking on the ground, often with a bike or scooter icon inside. Parking in one is ideal — call it out positively if detected.

5. Vehicles must not block streets, driveways, or public transport stops.

6. Parking near crosswalks, ramps, or handicap-accessible areas is not allowed.

Use emojis to open your feedback. Do not mention the brand — refer only to "scooter" or "e-bike".
prompt_2:
Analyze this parking photo of a shared micromobility vehicle. Rate it as PASS or FAIL and provide feedback accordingly. Apply the following rules:

1. The vehicle must be mostly visible in the photo. Minor cropping is acceptable. The photo should include enough surrounding space to judge whether the vehicle is parked appropriately.
2. Vehicles must not block sidewalks or pedestrian paths. A vehicle parked to the side, near a wall, pillar, or building edge is generally not blocking pedestrian flow — only FAIL if the path is clearly obstructed.
3. Parking on sidewalks is ok if the vehicle is reasonably to the side and does not obstruct pedestrian flow. Give benefit of the doubt if there is visible walking space remaining.
4. Parking in the furniture zone of the sidewalk/street or some grass next to it is ok and better than if parked in the middle of the sidewalk.
5. Micromobility space is a white rectangle marking, often containing a white bike or scooter icon in its middle. Parking in designated micromobility spaces is preferred, but optional. Call out as positive if you detect this case.
6. Vehicles must not block streets, driveways, or public transport stations.
7. Parking near crosswalks, ramps, or handicap-accessible areas is not allowed. Only FAIL for this if the vehicle is clearly and directly blocking the ramp or crosswalk path — not merely nearby.
8. A vehicle lying on its side is not ideal but is not grounds for FAIL on its own.

When in any doubt, rule in favor of the rider. Only rate FAIL if you are clearly certain a rule is violated.

Condense your feedback into a single, short sentence. Use clear, helpful language and start feedback with an appropriate emoji.

Do not refer to rules by number. Do not refer to the brand (Bird, Spin), only the vehicle type (scooter, e-bike).
